channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta partners with horror movie maker Blumhouse Productions to test Movie Gen
item.description: Facebook owner Meta said on Thursday it had teamed up with Blumhouse Productions, the Hollywood company behind popular horror films such as The Purge and Get Out, to put its new generative AI video model Movie Gen to the test.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Acumatica tweaks its cloud ERP for professional services firms
item.description: Acumatica has released a new “edition” of its cloud-based enterprise resource planning (ERP) software tailored to the needs of mid-size professional services firms. The number of potential customers for Acumatica’s new functionality — or similar products from competitors — is huge.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Treasury Department uses AI to recover $1 billion in fraud
item.description: The Treasury Department is taking advantage of artificial intelligence (AI) to prevent or recover check fraud and stopped more than $1 billion in fraudulent payments over the past year, the agency announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kroger's Use of Facial Recognition Technology Raises Concerns
item.description: Kroger's use of facial recognition technology at grocery stores has raised concerns about discrimination and price gouging.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Kernstine, NewsNation

item.title: From Excel to AI: How Liberty Dental revolutionized care management
item.description: Liberty Dental Plan insures about 7 million people in the United States as a dental insurance company. We are owned by a private equity company and Anthem, which is now called Elevance Health. Our job is to take care of all 7 million members of our members.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to use Task.WhenEach in .NET 9
item.description: The upcoming release of .NET 9 introduces the Task.WhenEach method, which enables developers to handle asynchronous tasks more elegantly. The Task.WhenEach method allows us to iterate through tasks as they complete, without having to call Task.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How Libya and Tuvalu are cashing in on their top-level domain names
item.description: Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it’s not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: DoNotPay will now call customer service hotlines for you
item.description: If you dread the thought of calling to change an airline ticket or negotiate your internet bill, a new artificial intelligence tool may provide a solution.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: Ride-hailing service Lyft announced it will offer discounted rides, bikeshare and scooters on Election Day, an initiative designed to increase transportation access for voters, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk loaded $75 million into his pro-Trump super PAC in 3 months
item.description: Elon Musk has personally contributed at least $75 million to his pro-Trump America PAC since July as he pushes for the former president to return to the White House, according to campaign finance records filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Intel, AMD unite in new x86 alliance to tackle AI, other challenges
item.description: Semiconductor rivals Intel and AMD announced the formation of an x86-processor advisory group that will try to address ever-increasing AI workloads, custom chiplets, and advances in 3D packaging and system architectures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: It’s your last day to comment on one of the biggest cybersecurity rules ever
item.description: Final comments on a Defense Department cybersecurity rule are due today. The Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification project is about to become a reality. The post It’s your last day to comment on one of the biggest cybersecurity rules ever first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Open source package entry points could be used for command jacking
item.description: Open source application packages, including those in Python and JavaScript, have a vulnerability in their entry points that could be used by threat actors to execute malicious code to steal data, plant malware, and more.
item.dc:creator: csoonline
